title: rubus × eximius
intro: Rubus × eximius, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a cross between the western thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus) and the red raspberry (Rubus idaeus). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can help soothe and protect the skin. The thimbleberry has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
